title: Spread Period FAIL: PISANO_PERIOD_MISMATCH + MATRIX_PERIOD_WRONG — claimed period=12 for m=9
_fixture_purpose: Canonical fail fixture. For m=9, the correct Pisano period is π(9)=24. A common error is to claim period=12 because F^12=-I (projective order 12) rather than the linear order 24. SP2 fires: π(9)=24≠12. SP3 fires: F^12=[[8,0],[0,8]]=-I≢I mod 9. Both PISANO_PERIOD_MISMATCH and MATRIX_PERIOD_WRONG fire. SP4 does not fire for period=12 since checking minimality of claimed period 12: F^6=[[5,8],[8,4]]≢I → minimality would pass, but SP2 and SP3 already flag the error.

spread_period._error: WRONG: claimed period=12 (projective order in PGL₂(Z/9Z)) but correct linear order in GL₂(Z/9Z) is 24; Pisano period π(9)=24≠12; F^12=-I≢I

validation_checks.description: Fibonacci period mod m == claimed cosmos_period

validation_checks.details: F^6=[[5,8],[8,4]]≢I mod 9 → period 12 would be minimal IF F^12=I (but it isn't — caught by SP3)

validation_checks.description: orbit_type ∈ valid set AND period=1 ↔ singularity
